name: Agroecology Fund
description: US-based multi-donor philanthropic fund. Has awarded $41M+ to 756 grants in 102 countries since 2012. Deploys grants — not investment capital — to grassroots agroecology organizations and movements.

name: Fondo Agroecológico para la Península de Yucatán
description: First regional fund seeded by AEF, established 2020 with support from Kellogg Foundation. Operated by Túumben K'óoben Cooperative across Yucatán, Campeche, and Quintana Roo. On-grants to community organizations working in agroecological production, solidarity markets, biodiversity, and climate resilience. Acts as a learning community facilitator among grantees.
relationship_note: First regional fund seeded by AEF in 2020. AEF provided founding capital and ongoing structural support.

name: Bharat Agroecology Fund
description: Regional fund established 2022, hosted by Bharathiya Vikas Trust. Targets a tipping point: 20% of India's small and marginal farmers (~20 million people) shifting to agroecology by 2030. On-grants to grassroots movements while also catalyzing Indian philanthropy and moving public money into natural farming through government collaboration.
relationship_note: Seeded by AEF in 2022. AEF provided founding capital and hosts the fund's development toward independence.

name: Regional Agroecology Fund — Eastern Africa
description: Regional fund in incubation, seeded by AEF. On-grants to agroecology networks and grassroots organizations across Eastern Africa. Prioritizes Indigenous Peoples, women, and youth. Functions as a learning community — grantees share strategies and document impact to build a regional evidence base. Responds to the failure of Green Revolution inputs to address poverty, malnutrition, and soil degradation.
relationship_note: Currently in incubation, seeded by AEF as part of its regional fund model.

name: Regional Agroecology Fund — West Africa
description: Regional fund in incubation, seeded by AEF. On-grants to agroecology networks and organizations across West Africa. Offers donors a direct channel to hard-to-reach grassroots organizations. Part of a growing movement rejecting Green Revolution inputs in favor of millenia-old local practices and agroecological renaissance.
relationship_note: Currently in incubation, seeded by AEF as part of its regional fund model.

name: La Via Campesina / GRAIN / ETC Group
description: Long-term strategic partnership since 2012, continuously renewed through 2025. The core peasant food sovereignty movement globally. Joint grantee partnership defending and amplifying peasant-led agroecology.
relationship_note: Continuous partnership since 2012, renewed through 2025. Core strategic relationship with the global peasant food sovereignty movement.

name: Alliance for Food Sovereignty in Africa
description: Multi-year partner since 2014. Mobilizes African civil society for agroecology, influencing decision makers and funders. Active in Ethiopia, Senegal, Kenya, Uganda, Ghana, Rwanda, Tunisia, Nigeria, and more.
relationship_note: Multi-year partner since 2014. One of AEF's primary vehicles for movement-building across Africa.

name: Groundswell International
description: Partner since 2013. Scales farmer-led agroecology in Burkina Faso, Ghana, Mali, and Senegal through women's empowerment and advocacy. Currently in second phase of Agroecology for Systems Change in the Sahel.
relationship_note: Partner since 2013. Long-term investment in farmer-led agroecology scaling in West Africa.

name: PELUM Association
description: Participatory Ecological Land Use Management. Multi-year partner with Kenya, Zimbabwe, and regional chapters. Supports participatory farmer-led research and inclusive agroecology policies across Eastern and Southern Africa.
relationship_note: Multi-year partner. Ongoing support for participatory land use and agroecology policy across Eastern and Southern Africa.

name: International Indian Treaty Council
description: Partner since 2016. Indigenous Peoples Alliance for Food Sovereignty, Traditional Knowledge and Climate Change. Active in US, Mexico, Central and South America, and the Pacific.
relationship_note: Partner since 2016. AEF's primary Indigenous Peoples alliance for food sovereignty and traditional knowledge.

name: Grassroots International
description: Early partner since 2012. Also listed as an AEF donor. Catalyst for expanding the global agroecology movement across Brazil, Mexico, India, South Africa, Nepal, and the US.
relationship_note: Unique reciprocal relationship — both an early partner since 2012 and an AEF donor. One of the few organizations with a dual role in the ecosystem.

name: Nous Sommes la Solution
description: Partner since 2016. Builds rural women's movement for food sovereignty, agroecology, and gender justice across Burkina Faso, Ghana, Guinea, Mali, and Senegal. Promotes traditional rice biodiversity.
relationship_note: Partner since 2016. Long-term investment in rural women's movement-building for food sovereignty in West Africa.

name: Articulação Nacional de Agroecologia
description: Brazil's national agroecology network. Current grant supports agroecology networks in response to climate change through action research from the ground up.
relationship_note: Ongoing support for Brazil's national agroecology network and climate-responsive action research.

name: AS-PTA Agricultura Familiar e Agroecologia
description: Brazilian advocacy organization connecting agroecology practice to policy. Works to defend healthy food and food sovereignty through farmer networks and policy influence.

name: El Colegio de la Frontera Sur
description: Mexican research institution. Hosts AEF's IPA Global participatory research initiative. Runs Latin American professional Master's program in agroecology and facilitates participatory action research across the region.
relationship_note: Institutional host of IPA Global since its founding. Continuous structural partner for participatory research in Latin America.

name: Centre for Agroecology, Water and Resilience
description: Coventry University research centre. Partner since 2014. Contributed to agroecology for sustainable food systems in Europe and Tripura agroecology partnership in India.

name: IPA Global (Initiative for Participatory Research in Agroecology)
description: AEF's own special initiative for participatory research. Coordinated through ECOSUR. Builds grassroots evidence base for agroecology through action research with farmer organizations.
relationship_note: AEF-created initiative coordinated through ECOSUR. A structural investment in building grassroots evidence for agroecology.

name: Grassroots Evidence for Agroecology
description: AEF special initiative offering a free online course and evidence platform documenting grassroots agroecology impact. Contributes to a global body of evidence for policy influence.
relationship_note: AEF-created initiative. A structural investment in documenting and disseminating grassroots agroecology impact globally.

name: A Growing Culture
description: Strengthens grassroots advocacy for agroecology and food sovereignty. Current grant supports seeds of change advocacy work globally.

name: International Planning Committee for Food Sovereignty
description: Global multi-stakeholder platform for food sovereignty. Current grant supports the 3rd Nyeleni Global Forum. Connects peasant movements with UN policy processes.

name: European Coordination Via Campesina
description: Multi-year partner. Maps agroecology across Central Asia and Eastern Europe. Leverages finance and trade policies in European institutions for the global agroecology movement.

name: National Family Farm Coalition
description: US policy and advocacy network. Current grant supports farmland access for farmers as the bedrock of resilient food systems.

name: National Black and Food Justice Alliance
description: Advancing agroecology and Black agrarians as climate and culture catalysts. Current grant supports recognition of Black farmers' unsung contributions to food systems transformation.

name: Pesticide Action Network North America
description: Policy advocacy for sustainable pest management. Current grant supports enacting a Sustainable Pest Management roadmap in California as a model for other states.

name: Agroecology Coalition
description: 300+ member global policy network of governments, UN agencies, and civil society. AEF co-convenes donors through the Coalition and co-launched the Agroecology Investment Tracking Tool in 2023.
relationship_note: AEF co-convenes the donor community through the Coalition. No grant relationship — a peer collaboration among equals.

name: 11th Hour Project
description: Schmidt Family Foundation program focused on a just and sustainable food and energy system.

name: 1% for the Planet
description: Global network of businesses and individuals that donate 1% of sales to environmental causes.

name: American Jewish World Service
description: International human rights organization funding social change in the Global South.

name: Arcus Foundation
description: Foundation focused on social justice and conservation, including great ape protection and related agroecology connections.

name: Ballmer Group
description: Philanthropic organization focused on economic mobility for children and families in the US.

name: Ceres Trust
description: Foundation supporting sustainable agriculture, food systems, and environmental health.
donor_note: Now closed; spent down assets. Data reflects period as active AEF donor.

name: CS Fund
description: Family foundation focused on social and environmental change.

name: David Rockefeller Fund
description: Foundation focused on environment, democratic values, and racial equity.

name: David & Lucile Packard Foundation
description: Major foundation supporting conservation, science, and local grantmaking including food and water.

name: Dunn Family Charitable Foundation
description: Family foundation supporting agroecology and food systems change.

name: Stichting Doen
description: Dutch foundation funding culture, nature, and a social and green economy.

name: Mize Family Foundation
description: Family foundation supporting agroecology and sustainable food systems.

name: Grassroots International
description: Also an AEF anchor partner. Funds global grassroots movements for food sovereignty and agroecology.

name: IDRC — International Development Research Centre
description: Canadian government agency funding research in developing regions. Bilateral development donor.

name: IKEA Foundation
description: Philanthropic foundation focused on climate, renewable energy, and sustainable livelihoods for farming families.

name: John & Marcia Goldman Foundation
description: Family foundation focused on food systems, environment, and social equity.

name: Sall Family Foundation
description: Family foundation supporting sustainable food and agriculture.

name: New England Biolabs Foundation
description: Foundation funding sustainable agriculture, environment, and science education.

name: Oak Foundation
description: International foundation addressing social and environmental issues. Funds food and agriculture among other areas.

name: One Earth
description: Philanthropic organization focused on climate solutions including regenerative agriculture and biodiversity.

name: Panta Rhea Foundation
description: Foundation supporting sustainable food systems, land stewardship, and ecological health.

name: Synchronicity Earth
description: UK foundation funding biodiversity conservation and overlooked environmental causes.

name: Savitri (Haslam Family Charitable Trust)
description: Charitable trust supporting agroecology and sustainable food systems.

name: Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation
description: Swiss federal agency for development cooperation. Key bilateral donor representing government engagement with AEF.

name: Shockwave Foundation
description: Foundation funding multi-sectoral agroecology movements globally.

name: Stiftelsen Værekraft
description: Norwegian foundation supporting agroecology and food sovereignty.

name: McKnight Foundation
description: Major US foundation. Food program supports resilient and sustainable food systems including agroecology in the US and internationally.

name: Tauck Family Foundation
description: Family foundation supporting agroecology and environmental causes.

name: Casey & Family Foundation
description: Family foundation supporting agroecology and food systems.

name: Godley Family Foundation
description: Family foundation supporting sustainable agriculture and agroecology.

name: W.K. Kellogg Foundation
description: Major US foundation. Funded FAPY's establishment and supports food systems transformation. Key institutional donor.

name: Thread Fund
description: Fund supporting agroecology and sustainable food systems.

name: SWF Immersion Foundation
description: Foundation supporting agroecology and food systems.

name: Wallace Global Fund
description: Foundation focused on advancing social equity and environmental sustainability including food systems.

name: Waverleyst Foundation
description: Foundation supporting agroecology and sustainable food systems.

name: Woka Foundation
description: Foundation supporting agroecology movements globally.

name: Christopher Reynolds Foundation
description: Foundation focused on sustainable agriculture and international development.

name: Katsura Fund
description: Fund supporting agroecology and food sovereignty.
donor_note: Ecosystem description mentions food sovereignty; AE explicitness not independently confirmed.

name: Porticus
description: International philanthropy organization (Brenninkmeijer family) funding sustainable food systems and social initiatives globally.

name: Nina Alexandersen
description: Individual philanthropist supporting agroecology.
